case_title: CARLO FLORES BOLANOS VS. JOSE BICHAO CANTE

docket_entries.description: SUMMONS ISSUED (TRANSACTION ID # 67243596) TO PLAINTIFF BOLANOS, CARLO FLORES

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS JOSE CANTE AND JESUS BICHAO-CANTE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 435.5, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A MOTION TO STRIKE IS FILED. A DECLARATION MUST THEN BE FILED WHICH SPECIFIES WHETHER THE MEET AND CONFER WAS IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDENT'S RESPONSE TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY 11/25/22. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Off Calendar May-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF CARLO BOLANOS' Motion To Vacate Dismissal On The Ground Of Mistake, In Advertence, Surprise And/Or Excusable Neglect.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

John E. Cowan, Esq., Law Offices of, (888) 252-1231, for Plaintiff.

No other appearances.

This hearing does not require a stipulation as the judge pro tem presiding this morning previously issued the order that is the subject of Plaintiff's pending motion.

Not argued/contested. At the outset, the court advises counsel that after further review and consideration of Plaintiff's pleadings, it intends to change its tentative ruling and grant Plaintiff's subject motion. The court shall prepare and issue an order thereon.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Continued To Jan-18-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion In Support Of Defendants Motion To Strike Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Oct-24-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JOSE CANTE and JESUS BICHAO-CANTE'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 435.5, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a motion to strike is filed. A declaration must then be filed which specifies whether the meet and confer was in person or by telephone. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Defendent's response to the first amended complaint is now due by 11/25/22.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S JOSE CANTE and JESUS BICHAO-CANTE'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Jose Cante's and Jose Bichao-Cante's motion to strike is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 435.45, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a motion to strike is filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by October 24, 2022.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
